title: Daylight provision, Atlanta IBC §1010
kicker: Why this opening is the size it is

reasoning.text: Atlanta 2026 IBC §1010 wants at least 10 sq ft of glazing for every 200 sq ft of habitable floor area. That works out to a 5% ratio.
The opening we measured here is roughly 8.4 sq ft. The floor area is 198 sq ft. The ratio comes out to 4.2%.
About 1.6 sq ft short. Respec the window, add a transom, or file for a §1009.4 variance.

title: FAR remaining on this parcel
kicker: How much more it can hold

reasoning.text: The parcel sits in MR-3 on the Atlanta DPCD zoning atlas, which caps FAR at 0.8.
With this addition the build is at 0.7 FAR.
Roughly 0.1 FAR of room left, about 3,200 sq ft, for whatever comes next.

title: MARTA TOD bonus, eligible
kicker: Transit overlay you can opt into

reasoning.text: Site is within a half mile of MARTA rail, which qualifies it for the TOD overlay.
TOD opens up a 0.3 FAR bonus and drops parking minimums to half a space per unit.
If you take it, that's roughly 9,600 more sq ft on this parcel.

title: Sidewalk frontage and pedestrian access
kicker: What's happening on the street side

reasoning.text: Frontage on this side is about 60 ft. Existing sidewalk is roughly 5 ft wide with no planted buffer.
Atlanta's complete-streets policy expects 6 ft of sidewalk plus a planted buffer in this district.
Walkscore for the block sits around 78. Nearest signalized crossing is 40 ft south. Most foot traffic here uses a desire line instead of the crossing.

title: Census tract the parcel sits in

reasoning.text: Median household income in the tract is around $58k. About 22% of residents are 65 or older. 14% are below the poverty line.
Affordability and walkability both matter for the use mix you can put here.
This is the layer that ties the building, the street, and the tract together. None of the existing tools do all three in one place.
